headline: Will AT&T’s (T) Fiber Momentum and Legacy Exit Plan Reshape Its Long-Term Connectivity Narrative?
summary: In June 2026, the FCC’s Wireline Competition Bureau invited public comments on AT&T’s plan to discontinue certain legacy voice services, while AT&T also announced a year-end CFO transition and continued rolling out fiber and next-generation 9-1-1 enhancements with partners such as Intrado. Independent research naming AT&T Fiber a leading home internet performer across speed, reliability, and video quality adds fresh evidence that its long-term pivot away from legacy networks toward...

headline: Elon Musk's SpaceX Could Acquire T-Mobile In Wireless Push, Says Analyst
summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX could acquire a U.S. wireless services provider, with T-Mobile the most likely target, speculated a TD Cowen analyst.

headline: Q1 Rundown: AT&T (NYSE:T) Vs Other Consumer Discretionary - Wireless, Cable and Satellite Stocks
summary: The end of the earnings season is always a good time to take a step back and see who shined (and who didn’t). Let’s take a look at how consumer discretionary - wireless, cable and satellite stocks fared in Q1, starting with AT&T (NYSE:T).

headline: AT&T (T) Trades Near Its 52-Week Low, But Analysts Remain Bullish
summary: With 72 hedge funds holding bullish positions and analysts seeing 40.27% upside potential, AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T) ranks among the top stocks to buy for financial stability. Despite recent share-price weakness, analysts remain constructive on AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T), pointing to the company’s position in the evolving convergence trend across the U.S. telecom and cable industry. At […]

headline: AI chip market the 'hottest and riskiest' to play: Ray Wang
summary: Ray Wang, Principal Analyst & Founder of Constellation Research, analyses Micron's latest earnings. He says the world will be seeing memory shortage for the next 24 months, making the chip market the hottest and riskiest for trading.
